perturbation.original_text: "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to each Party, such efforts and commitment of resources in accordance with [***] that such Party [***]. As used in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts", "reasonable" shall be measured by [***]. References in this Agreement to "commercially reasonable" and similar formulations shall be deemed to incorporate the standard set forth in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts";
perturbation.changed_text: "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to each Party, such efforts and commitment of resources as deemed appropriate by said party in its sole discretion, regardless of industry standards or specific requirements. References in this Agreement to "commercially reasonable" and similar formulations shall be deemed to incorporate the standard set forth in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts";
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' references external standards ([***]) for measuring reasonableness. The modified definition replaces this with 'as deemed appropriate by said party in its sole discretion,' eliminating the objective standard. This contradicts Section 3.6(d), which requires Antares to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to replenish the Safety Stock, implying a measurable effort, not simply what Antares subjectively considers reasonable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Antares or its Subcontractor shall manage the Safety Stock as part of its overall inventory and use the Safety Stock to fulfill its obligations pursuant to a Firm Orders on a first in/first out basis. As such inventory of Safety Stock is used as part of the Manufacturing Services of Product and/or sample Product, Antares shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to replenish the Safety Stock to the level set forth in Section 3.6(a)(i) (as adjusted pursuant to Section 3.6(a)(ii)) within [***] of receipt of such Firm Order.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' allowed for an objective measurement based on external standards. The changed definition grants the party sole discretion, removing any objective benchmark. This directly contradicts Section 3.6(d), which requires Antares to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to replenish the Safety Stock, implying a measurable and objective effort. Antares could now argue that any level of effort (or lack thereof) is 'commercially reasonable' at its sole discretion, rendering the obligation in Section 3.6(d) meaningless and unenforceable. The altered definition breaks compatibility with the requirement for Antares to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts,' creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.8 Validation Activities. Antares or its Subcontractor will be responsible for the development and approval of the validation protocols for analytical methods and manufacturing processes (including packaging processes) for the Products, sample Products and Trainers as described in the Specifications in accordance with the Quality Agreement and shall be approved by AMAG prior to execution thereof. [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 2.8 Validation Activities. Antares or its Subcontractor will be solely responsible for the development and approval of the validation protocols for analytical methods and manufacturing processes (including packaging processes) for the Products, sample Products and Trainers without any approval from AMAG needed. [***].
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires AMAG's approval of validation protocols before execution. The modified text eliminates this requirement, stating Antares is 'solely responsible' and 'without any approval from AMAG needed'. This directly contradicts Section 2.6(a), which mandates a Quality Agreement and implies a collaborative approach to quality control and assurance, including validation activities. Further this contradict the same section where it is being reffered about the quality agreement that involves the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.6 Quality Control and Quality Assurance. (a) On or about the date hereof, the Parties shall amend and restate the Quality Agreement entered into on May 16, 2016 between the Parties covering the Product, sample Products, Trainers, the Device and the Prefilled Syringes, as set forth in the form of Amended and Restated Quality Agreement attached hereto as Exhibit E (as amended and restated, the "Quality Agreement"). The Parties shall review the Quality Agreement and shall modify the same from time to time as detailed in the Quality Agreement as necessary through a written amendment to the Quality Agreement signed by an authorized representative on behalf of each of the Parties. The Parties shall perform the quality control and quality assurance testing specified in Section 2.6(b) and the Quality Agreement. The Parties shall perform Product, sample Product and Trainer review and final release of the Product, sample Product and Trainers for sale in accordance with Section 2.6(b) and the Quality Agreement, the Specifications and Applicable Laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Antares is solely responsible for validation protocols 'without any approval from AMAG needed'. This directly conflicts with Section 2.6(a), which mandates a Quality Agreement ('Exhibit E') between the parties, implying a collaborative approach to quality control and assurance, including validation activities that necessitate both parties' consent. The original text requiring AMAG's approval of validation protocols aligns with the quality agreement, and the changed text eliminates this requirement, contradicting the need for collaborative quality control as laid out in 2.6(a).

perturbation.original_text: AMAG shall promptly notify Antares of any Third Person Claim upon becoming aware thereof, and shall permit Antares at Antares' cost to defend against such Third Person Claim and to control the defense and disposition (including, without limitation, selection its counsel and all decisions to litigate, settle or appeal) of such Third Person Claim and shall cooperate in the defense thereof. AMAG may, at its option and expense, have its own counsel participate in any proceeding that is under the direction of Antares and will cooperate with Antares or its insurer in the disposition of any such matter.
perturbation.changed_text: Antares retains absolute authority over any decisions related to any potential Third Person Claim arising out of this contract. Antares shall inform AMAG of any Third Person Claim upon becoming aware thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants Antares control over the defense and disposition of Third Person Claims, after AMAG notifies Antares of any such claim. The changed text gives Antares 'absolute authority' over decisions, but removes AMAG's right to 'permit Antares at Antares' cost to defend against such Third Person Claim and to control the defense and disposition'. This contradicts Section 9.2 which explicitly lays out AMAG's right to defend against third person claims under specific conditions. Now it could be interpreted, that Antares can override this defense.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AMAG's Right to Indemnification. Antares shall indemnify each of AMAG, its Affiliates, and their respective successors and assigns, and the directors, officers, employees, and agents thereof (the "AMAG Indemnitees"), defend and hold each AMAG Indemnitee harmless from and against any and all Damages incurred by or asserted against any AMAG Indemnitee of whatever kind or nature, including, without limitation, any claim or liability based upon negligence, warranty, strict liability, violation of government regulation or infringement of patent or other proprietary rights, but only to the extent arising from or occurring as a result of a Third Person Claim against any AMAG Indemnitee because of (a) breach of any warranty made by Antares pursuant to Section 8.3 hereof; (b) any alleged defect in the design or functionality of the Device; (c) the failure by Antares or its Subcontractors to provide the Manufacturing Services according to Specifications, except to the extent AMAG approved such failure pursuant to its in process acceptance activities set forth in the Quality Agreement; (d) [***]; (e) the warehousing or shipping of a Product, sample Product or Trainer by Antares, except to the extent such claim alleges infringement of any patent, other intellectual property rights or other proprietary rights of a Third Person; (f) any allegation that the Manufacturing Services performed under this Agreement or the manufacture, use, sale, offer for sale or importation of a Device (including a Device incorporated into a Product) or any delivery system including the Device, in such cases, infringes any patent, other intellectual property rights or other proprietary rights of a Third Person; or (g) any breach of this Agreement by Antares, except, in each such case, to the extent that such Damages are finally determined to have resulted from the negligence or misconduct of AMAG or a sublicensee of AMAG. AMAG shall promptly notify Antares of any Third Person Claim upon becoming aware thereof, and shall permit Antares at Antares' cost to defend against such Third Person Claim and to control the defense and disposition (including, without limitation, selection its counsel and all decisions to litigate, settle or appeal) of such Third Person Claim and shall cooperate in the defense thereof. AMAG may, at its option and expense, have its own counsel participate in any proceeding that is under the direction of Antares and will cooperate with Antares or its insurer in the disposition of any such matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants Antares 'absolute authority' over Third Person Claims and removes AMAG's right to defend against such claims, contradicting the original text, which explicitly allows AMAG to defend against third-party claims under specific conditions as stated in the contradicted text 'AMAG shall promptly notify Antares of any Third Person Claim upon becoming aware thereof, and shall permit Antares at Antares' cost to defend against such Third Person Claim and to control the defense and disposition...'. By giving Antares absolute authority and removing AMAG's explicit right, a clear contradiction is created.
